content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 21, 2001

David Keller, Councilmember
City of Patterson
729 No. Third Street
Patterson, CA 95363

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-100

Dear Mr. Keller:

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of May 17, 2001, in which you informed me that the decision about which you requested advice has already been made. Because we do not advise with respect to past conduct, we decline to provide advice on this matter. You have indicated that there is another decision related to this matter that will be coming before the city council, and that you will submit a new request for advice on that matter. We hope that we may be of assistance on that matter.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

HBA:jg

sections.conclusion: Because we do not advise with respect to past conduct, we decline to provide advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC would provide advice regarding a decision that had already been made by the city council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the decision in question has already been made, as the FPPC does not advise on past conduct. However, the FPPC indicates willingness to advise on a related future decision that will come before the city council.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Document appears to follow up on a telephone conversation. Advice was declined due to timing (past conduct). FPPC expressed willingness to advise on future related matter.

CONCLUSION: Because we do not advise with respect to past conduct, we decline to provide advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice to Councilmember David Keller regarding a decision that has already been made by the city council, consistent with its policy of not advising on past conduct. The FPPC indicates availability to advise on a related future decision.
